# agentic-deployment

## Summary
A pnpm monorepo that provides a plugin-based agentic execution platform: a plugin SDK defines the plugin contract, a code-engine hosts and executes plugins at runtime, an Express API server exposes them over HTTP, and a React/Vite dashboard provides a minimal UI. The project is early-stage (all versions at 0.0.0).

## Tech Stack
| Layer | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| Language | TypeScript 5.8 (strict, ES2022, NodeNext modules) |
| Framework | Express 4 (API), React 19 (dashboard) |
| Package manager | pnpm 11.5 (workspace monorepo) |
| Build / bundler | `tsc` for library packages; Vite 6 for web-dashboard |
| Test runner | Not detected |
| Linter / formatter | Not detected |

## Key Commands
| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `pnpm build` | Build all packages recursively |
| `pnpm typecheck` | Typecheck all packages recursively |
| `pnpm start:as` | Start API server in dev mode (`tsx`, auto-builds deps first) |
| `pnpm start:wd` | Start web-dashboard Vite dev server |
| `pnpm --filter <pkg> run build` | Build a single package |
| `pnpm --filter <pkg> run typecheck` | Typecheck a single package |

> **Build order matters.** `api-server` `predev`/`prestart` hooks build `code-engine` and `test-plugin` first.

## Directory Structure
```
agentic-deployment/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ packages/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ api-server/       # Express HTTP server, plugin host entry point
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ code-engine/      # Plugin registry + execution engine
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ plugin-sdk/       # Minimal Plugin interface (shared contract)
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ web-dashboard/    # React + Vite frontend
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ plugins/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ test-plugin/      # Example plugin (git-clone implementation)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tsconfig.json         # Root TS config (extended by all packages)
в””в”Ђв”Ђ pnpm-workspace.yaml   # Workspace: packages/*, plugins/*
```

### Module Guide
- **`packages/plugin-sdk/`** вЂ” exports the `Plugin` type: `{ name: string; execute(args) }`. All plugins must satisfy this interface.
- **`packages/code-engine/`** вЂ” `createEngine()` returns an `Engine` with `register`, `getPlugin`, `getRegistry`, and `execute`. Validates plugins on registration and wraps execution errors in typed `EngineError` objects.
- **`packages/api-server/`** вЂ” Express server (default port 3000). `POST /api/ping` runs the first registered plugin with the request body as args. Currently hard-codes `gitPlugin`.
- **`packages/web-dashboard/`** вЂ” Single-button React app that POSTs `{ repoUrl }` to the API. Configurable via `VITE_API_URL` and `VITE_REPO_URL` env vars.
- **`plugins/test-plugin/`** вЂ” Implements `gitPlugin` (runs `git clone` via `execFileSync`) and a trivial `testPlugin` echo plugin.

## Architecture
```
web-dashboard  в”Ђв”ЂPOST /api/pingв”Ђв”Ђв–є  api-server
                                        в”‚
                                    code-engine (Engine)
                                        в”‚  register / execute
                                    plugin-sdk (Plugin interface)
                                        в”‚
                                    test-plugin (gitPlugin, testPlugin)
```
Plugins are registered at server startup. The engine validates the plugin shape, stores it in a `Map`, and dispatches calls by name. Errors flow back as structured `EngineError` objects with a typed `code` field.

## Code Style & Conventions
- **Language / strictness**: TypeScript strict mode + `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`; target ES2022, NodeNext module resolution.
- **Formatting**: No formatter config detected вЂ” follow existing code style (2-space indent, no trailing commas visible).
- **Imports**: NodeNext requires explicit `.js` extensions on relative imports (e.g. `"./engine.js"`). Import order per user rules: builtin в†’ external в†’ internal (`@agentic-deployment/*`) в†’ parent в†’ sibling в†’ type imports last.
- **Naming**: camelCase for variables/functions, PascalCase for types. Plugin files use kebab-case (e.g. `git-plugin.ts`). Source files live in `src/`, compiled output in `dist/`.
- **Packages**: Each library package has two tsconfigs вЂ” `tsconfig.json` (typecheck only, `noEmit`) and `tsconfig.build.json` (emits to `dist/`). `exports` in `package.json` points to `dist/`.
- **Error handling**: Errors are returned as typed `EngineError` values (not thrown) from the engine layer. Plugin `execute` can throw; the engine catches and wraps into `{ success: false, error }`.
- **Testing**: No test framework detected.
